scenario: Vanta Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with a fleet of 40 Spring Boot workers that process 25000 jobs per day across regional warehouses. The team is reviewing the default timeout under camunda.client.worker.defaults and must confirm how long each activated job is exclusively assigned to a worker by default.
question: What is the default value of camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout documented in the 8.8 properties reference?

options.text: The 8.8 reference lists PT5M as the default timeout, so an activated job is exclusively assigned to the worker for 5 minutes by default.
The 8.8 reference lists PT1M as the default timeout, so an activated job is exclusively assigned to the worker for 1 minute by default.
The 8.8 reference lists PT30S as the default timeout, so an activated job is exclusively assigned to the worker for 30 seconds by default.
The 8.8 reference lists PT15M as the default timeout, so an activated job is exclusively assigned to the worker for 15 minutes by default.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.worker.defaults section lists timeout with a default of PT5M; the value is the time a job remains exclusively assigned to the worker.

b.text: Incorrect. PT1M is not the documented default for timeout under camunda.client.worker.defaults; the reference lists PT5M.

c.text: Incorrect. PT30S is the default for poll-interval-style values, not for timeout; timeout defaults to PT5M in 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. PT15M is not the documented default; the reference table lists PT5M for camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout in 8.8.

explanation: The camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout property defaults to PT5M in the 8.8 Spring Boot starter and controls how long a job remains exclusively assigned to a single worker after activation.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
